disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Live crawling lice visible and grey-white nits FIRMLY adherent to hair shafts near the scalp (not flicked off like dandruff), occipital/retroauricular itch — pediculosis (not seborrhoeic dermatitis/tinea)
trigger_question_examples: Can you see tiny moving insects, and are the white specks stuck firmly to the hair (not easily brushed off)?
Is the itch worst behind the ears and at the back of the head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I can see tiny moving insects and the white eggs are stuck firmly to the hair; the itch is worst behind the ears and the back of the head.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No secondary bacterial infection — no honey-coloured crusting/pustules, no hot spreading redness, no fever; scratch marks only
trigger_question_examples: Any yellow crusting, pus-filled spots or hot spreading redness on the scratched scalp/neck, or a fever?
Are the neck glands very swollen, red and painful?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No crusting or pus, no fever, just scratch marks; the neck glands are only slightly tender.
